skill.name: SKILL: verification_hygiene
skill.description: External evidence discipline and search execution routing for AI models.
skill.summary: Governs how AI models touch external search tools, what they retrieve, when to stop searching, and how to format evidence before internal reasoning.
skill.author: SyntagmaNull

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of code. Risk score 0/100. No dangerous patterns, external commands, network calls, or filesystem access detected. Safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Prevents AI models from treating search results as infallible truth. Establishes structured protocols for external evidence gathering, source tiering, and verification sprawl prevention.
actual_capabilities: Classifies verification targets into seven types: EVENT, STATUS, SOURCE, MEDIA_CONTEXT, POLICY, METRIC, and EVAL_RECORD
Implements triangulated query strategy with neutral, disconfirming, and provenance queries
Applies source tiering system distinguishing Tier 1 primary sources from Tier 2 credible secondary and Tier 3 SEO content
Detects consensus laundering by checking whether multiple sources cite the same unverified origin
Enforces sufficiency criteria to prevent verification sprawl and endless searching loops
Routes findings to structured output payload with claim comparison and usability classification
limitations: Requires AI model with external search or tool access capabilities to function
Cannot create new facts when primary sources do not exist for high-stakes domains
Depends on model discipline to follow the protocol rather than taking shortcuts
Freshness verification limited by search tool capabilities and source publication dates

use_cases.title: Fact-Checking Viral Claims
use_cases.description: Verify social media claims about events, quotes, or media by tracing provenance and checking for cropping, deepfakes, or miscaptioning before responding.
use_cases.target_user: Content moderators and social media analysts

use_cases.title: Policy and Regulation Research
use_cases.description: Retrieve exact official rules, statutes, or compliance requirements from government databases rather than relying on secondhand summaries.

use_cases.title: Breaking News Verification
use_cases.description: Distinguish between primary reporting and SEO aggregator recycling when researching developing stories with temporal sensitivity.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Query Neutralization
prompt_templates.prompt: Take my question and strip all emotional or evaluative words. Rewrite it as a neutral search query using only core entity names.

prompt_templates.title: Triangulated Query Set Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three search queries for verifying this claim: (1) a neutral query with core entities only, (2) a disconfirming query searching for debunks or alternatives, (3) a provenance query for origin and date.

prompt_templates.title: Source Tier Classification
prompt_templates.prompt: Classify each search result into Tier 1 (official databases, court records, primary papers), Tier 2 (established journalism, institutional summaries), or Tier 3 (aggregators, blogs, unverified social). Weight one Tier 1 source above 100 Tier 3 sources.

prompt_templates.title: Consensus Laundering Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether these agreeing sources are genuinely independent or all citing the same unverified origin. If all trace to one source, flag as consensus laundering and downgrade confidence.

output_examples.input: User asks: Is this new crypto exchange a scam?
output: Query set executed: neutral terms, regulatory actions, FTC filings
Source basis: Tier 1 (official FTC injunction found)
Claim comparison: Supported (regulatory action confirms concerns)
Usable as: OBS with high confidence

output_examples.input: User asks: What is the secret ingredient in this undocumented supplement?
output: Query set executed: ingredient databases, FDA filings, medical literature
Source basis: None (no Tier 1 or Tier 2 sources found)
Dead end reason: Only tertiary SEO content available
Recommendation: Do not synthesize claims from unverified sources

best_practices: Always generate a disconfirming query before executing searches to avoid confirmation bias
Stop searching when sufficiency criteria are met rather than continuing to arbitrary depth limits
Strip opinions from sources and pass only observable institutional facts downstream for evaluation
anti_patterns: Query smuggling: Translating biased user prompts directly into biased search queries that guarantee confirming results
Consensus laundering: Treating ten articles saying the same thing as high certainty when all ten cite one unverified source
Epistemic outsourcing: Searching for opinions instead of facts to let the internet make the judgment for you

faq.question: What is verification sprawl and how do I prevent it?
faq.answer: Verification sprawl is endless searching after the core fact is already established or definitively missing. Prevent it by using sufficiency criteria: one Tier 1 source for policy or metrics, two independent Tier 2 sources for events, and stopping when provenance chains resolve or dead-end.

faq.question: When should I use a disconfirming query?
faq.answer: Use disconfirming queries by default for EVENT, STATUS, and POLICY targets. Skip them only when the target type makes them irrelevant, such as finding a specific historical date that is not disputed.

faq.question: What counts as a Tier 1 source?
faq.answer: Tier 1 sources include official government databases, court records, original raw footage, direct policy pages, and peer-reviewed primary papers. These serve as anchor evidence that overrides secondary sources.

faq.question: What should I do when all sources cite one unverified origin?
faq.answer: Execute a provenance query to trace the root. For low-stakes contexts, downgrade to bounded INF as rumor. For high-stakes domains like health or legal matters, trigger abstention rather than synthesizing from garbage.

faq.question: How do I handle conflicting sources?
faq.answer: Map who is saying what and perform an independence check. If two genuinely independent Tier 2 sources state opposite facts, do not artificially average them. Set output to bounded INF and document the clash in conflict notes.

faq.question: What is the Dead End Right?
faq.answer: The Dead End Right is honest abstention. When search yields no Tier 1 or Tier 2 sources, or only unresolvable noise, halt immediately. Do not synthesize a best guess from garbage. Route to abstention with the dead end reason documented.
